Title: goshs has CSRF in state-changing GET routes enables authenticated file deletion and directory creation
Description: goshs contains a cross-site request forgery issue in its state-changing HTTP GET routes. An external attacker can cause an already authenticated browser to trigger destructive actions such as `?delete` and `?mkdir` because goshs relies on HTTP basic auth alone and performs no CSRF, `Origin`, or `Referer` validation for those routes. I reproduced this on `v2.0.0-beta.5`.
NotImpacted: All versions before 2.0.0-beta.4, all versions starting from 2.0.0-beta.6
